content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnazium tracks provide optional early selection at ages 10–12, distinct from the OECD average of age 15, with approximately 85% of Hungarian students in schools with academic selection criteria.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Source does not quantify the actual proportion of students entering early tracks versus remaining in comprehensive schooling
OECD characterizes early selection as an 'equity risk' but does not provide causal impact estimates specific to Hungary
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
